figures.caption: Fig. 5. Time-domain Hz field responses in regions R1 and R2 for the artificial Lorentzian metasurface. (a)–(b) Matched configuration and (c)–(d) mismatched configuration. Observation points: (x1, y1) = (−0.1|c0τp|, 0) in R1 and (x2, y2) = (0.1|c0τp|, 0) in R2. Instantaneous relative error erel for the mismatched case for the: (e) reflected field H (1) z and (f) transmitted field H (2) z .
Fig. 1. (a) 2-D metasurface and (b) application of TD MAS GSTC. Black dots represent matching points on C, red (yellow) dots sources on C (1) aux (C (2) aux).

Fig. 2. (a) Temporal evolution of the boundary error for three different spatio-temporal resolutions (N1, N2,M,∆t). (b) Effect of auxiliary surface placement on numerical stability for N1 = N2 = 21, M = 85, and ∆t = 0.39∆t0.(c) Effect of dmp on the boundary error for N1 = N2 = 21, M = 85.

Fig. 4. Transient field components for the anisotropic black phosphorus metasurface: (a) Ez and (b) Hy in region R2 (transmitted fields), for both crystallographic orientations—armchair along y, zigzag along z (blue dots and lines) and the reverse (red dots and lines). Inset plots show the small difference due to anisotropy. Observation point: (x2, y2) = (0.1|xs|, 0) in R2. Instantaneous relative field error erel for the: (c) transmitted electric field E (2) z and (d) transmitted magnetic field H (2) y .

Fig. 3. Transient z-directed field components for the anisotropic graphene metasurface: (a) reflected E (1) z , (b) transmitted E (2) z , (c) reflected H (1) z , and (d) transmitted H (2) z . Fields are sampled at (x1, y1) = (−0.1|xs|, 0) in region R1 and (x2, y2) = (0.1|xs|, 0) in region R2. Instantaneous relative field error erel for the reflected (e) electric E (1) z and (f) magnetic H (1) z fields.
